LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING WHICH WAS NOT CONTESTED. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS IS SUSTAINED, WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFFS ARE GRANTED LEAVE TO AMEND, IF THEY CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, TO ALLEGE ADDITIONAL FACTS AND A STATUTORY BASIS TO ESTABLISH GOVERNMENT TORT LIABILITY AGAINST CCSF FOR THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENCE AND SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR PREMISES LIABILITY. (GOV. CODE 815.) THE COURT DECLINES TO RULE ON THE SUFFICIENCY OF THE ALLEGATIONS OF THE PROPOSED FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT AS THIS PLEADING IS OUTSIDE THE SCOPE OF THE INSTANT MOTION. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: SUSAN CROZIER, CSR # 4761

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION, AND SUSTAINED, WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION. FOR THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF HAS PLED SUFFICIENT FACTS TO ALLEGE A SPECIAL RELATIONSHIP BETWEEN PLAINTIFF AND DEFENDANT'S EMPLOYEES. (SEE ZELIG V. COUNTY OF LOS ANGELES (2002) 27 CAL.4TH 1112, 1129.) FOR THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFFS RELY ON GENERAL ALLEGATIONS, WHICH ARE INSUFFICIENT. (CERNA V. CITY OF OAKLAND (2008) 161 CAL.APP.4TH 1340, 1347.) PLAINTIFFS ARE GRANTED LEAVE TO AMEND THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION, IF THEY CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, TO SPECIFICALLY ALLEGE IN WHAT MANNER THE CONDITION CONSTITUTED A DANGEROUS CONDITION. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: RICHARD A. KRAMER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: TONY VAUGHN CSR6185.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-15-2013 Continued To Oct-30-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-12-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. rULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Overruled as to the first cause of action, and sustained, with 10 days leave to amend as to the second cause of action. For the first cause of action, Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts to allege a special relationship between Plaintiff and Defendant's employees. (See Zelig v. County of Los Angeles (2002) 27 Cal.4th 1112, 1129.) For the second cause of action, Plaintiffs rely on general allegations, which are insufficient. (Cerna v. City of Oakland (2008) 161 Cal.App.4th 1340, 1347.) Plaintiffs are granted leave to amend the second cause of action, if they can do so in good faith, to specifically allege in what manner the condition constituted a dangerous condition. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Richard A. Kramer, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Tony Vaughn CSR6185.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT

No appearance on this matter.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling which was not contested. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings is Sustained, with 10 days leave to amend. Plaintiffs are granted leave to amend, if they can do so in good faith, to allege additional facts and a statutory basis to establish government tort liability against CCSF for the first cause of action for negligence and second cause of action for premises liability. (Gov. Code 815.) The court declines to rule on the sufficiency of the allegations of the proposed First Amended Complaint as this pleading is outside the scope of the instant motion. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Susan Crozier, CSR # 4761
